description:
By removing the gdm package, the system no longer has GNOME installed. If X Windows is not installed then the system cannot boot into graphical user mode. This prevents the system from being accidentally or maliciously booted into a graphical.target mode. To do so, run the following command: 
$ sudo yum remove gdm
rationale:
Unnecessary service packages must not be installed to decrease the attack surface of the system.
A graphical environment is unnecessary for certain types of systems including a virtualization
hypervisor.
ocil:
To ensure the gdm package group is removed, run the following command: 
$ rpm -qi gdm
The output should be: 
package gdm is not installed
fixtext:
To remove the Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 package gdm run the following command:

$ sudo dnf remove gdm
srg_requirement: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 must not have the gdm package installed.

stig.srg_requirement: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 must not have the gdm  package installed.
stig.vuldiscussion:
Unnecessary service packages must not be installed to decrease the attack surface of the system.
A graphical environment is unnecessary for certain types of systems including a virtualization
hypervisor.
stig.checktext:
Verify that the gdm package is not installed with the following command:

$ dnf list --installed gdm

Error: No matching Packages to list

If the gdm  package is installed, this is a finding.
stig.fixtext:
Remove the gdm  package with the following command:

$ sudo dnf remove gdm

title: Remove the GDM Package Group
